tool_definitions.name: task_create
tool_definitions.description: Create a new task on the task board. Returns the created task. Use task_list to see all tasks, task_update to mark progress.

tool_definitions.name: task_get
tool_definitions.description: Retrieve full details for a specific task by ID, including metadata and timestamps. Use task_list to find task IDs.

tool_definitions.name: task_update
tool_definitions.description: Update a task's status, description, or progress text. Set status to 'in_progress' when starting work (only one task may be in_progress at a time). Set status to 'completed' with an output summary when done. Set status to 'failed' with a reason if the task errored. Set status to 'killed' to cancel. Update active_form to change the live progress text shown in the spinner.

tool_definitions.name: task_list
tool_definitions.description: List tasks on the board with optional status or assignee filters. Returns TaskSummary objects (use task_get for full details including metadata and timestamps). Results are ordered: in_progress first, then pending, then terminal states.

tool_definitions.name: task_stop
tool_definitions.description: Stop (kill) a currently in-progress task. Returns an error if the task is not in_progress (pending tasks do not need stopping — just don't start them). Downstream tasks that depend on this task will become unreachable.

tool_definitions.name: task_output
tool_definitions.description: Retrieve the output or current status of a task. Returns full TaskResult for completed tasks, status info for pending/in_progress tasks, and error details for failed/killed tasks.

tool_definitions.name: task_delegate
tool_definitions.description: Delegate a pending task to a named child agent. Records the intended executor in metadata.delegatedTo only — the task remains pending with no assignedTo change. The spawned worker claims the task via task_update(status: in_progress).

steps.message: MCP transport: connecting
MCP transport: connected
Use the task_create tool to create a task with subject "Review README" and description "Review the README for typos". Then use the task_list tool to show all tasks.
